title: Detonator Orgun and Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Detonator Orgun and Evangelion             Quote Post by xPearse » Mon Jul 14, 2014 4:22 pm I was talking to my brother today about the Evangelion proposal and said that the Eva's look like something from Detonator Orgun, we then talked about Orgun and it just hit me that Evangelion has a few similarities. First take a look at these pics, notice similarities in their design. http://vanishingtrooper.files.wordpress.com/2012/07/orgun.jpg http://collectiondx.com/files/orgunandtomoru.jpg http://tw.greywool.com/i/Wh9wP.jpg http://tw.greywool.com/i/vOsmM.jpg Yea I know these weren't used in the show but there's more, back when Orgun came out (1991-1993) many companies then copied designs and plot points into their own shows due to it's popularity. I think Gainax was one of those, im not out to generate any hate or point fingers. I just want to discuss this, I know they didn't take the mech designs but possibly they took influences from other areas. Here is a list of similar plot points, some are critical and a plot twist in Orgun. Must be piloted by someone with genetic link, Orgun his descendant and Evangelion her son. Both are humans not in human form, Orgun humans evolved into bipedal machines while Evangelion is organic. The same can be said for the villains, Angels nearly 100% human while Orgun it's similar. The pilot is absorbed into the mech and disintegrates, Orgun into the mech as pilot while Evangelion it happens eventually. Mechs are in humanoid design and move like people. Retrofit their own mechs from the enemy's while Evangelion it's Adam and Lilith. (It's in other stuff but I wanted to include it anyway) I remember talking about more stuff with my brother but can't fully recall it, anyhow what's your opinion on what's been gathered so far. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Detonator_Orgun

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 14, 2014 4:49 pm There's also a mention of it here: Tributes to Other Fiction in Neon Genesis Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Mon Jul 14, 2014 6:52 pm Didn't notice that one there, it has some of the points I brought up but not all. I would say Evangelion did take influences from Orgun espically seeing how similar the much designs are in the proposal.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jul 15, 2014 10:55 am That mention of Orgun doesn't mention anything of how the early Eva designs draw from Orgun's mecha, a very significant fact in itself. xPearse - well spotted!

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Tue Jul 15, 2014 3:34 pm Yea, I was just like "I know that design from somewhere" when I looked at the proposal. Especially the white Eva, Orgun was also white with a bit of red. There's also some other stuff that wasn't mentioned on the tributes page such as the necessary genetic link, a point needed to pilot the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Tue Jul 15, 2014 3:42 pm Feel free to improve my original entry. Just remember proper (heavy) hedging.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Wed Jul 16, 2014 1:13 am the robots from Orgun are way more top-heavy and cartoony looking than the Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jul 16, 2014 3:03 am The early designs of the Evas are more cartoony-looking than the final versions, although the original Eva designs have always been somewhat top-heavy. Just look at the shoulders!

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Wed Jul 16, 2014 7:19 am They're meant to resemble people encased in armor, most notably a knights plate armor. Hence forth the white knight color and symbolism, although that was pulled from Casshan as Orgun has many references to it. I wouldn't say they look cartoony at all, as I said armor. Its just a different design, I love the top heavy shoulders. Pretty awesome, Magic Knight Rayearth also took designs from Orgun and that's why the Rune Gods look amazing. One of my favorite mecha designs, I just felt like mentioning that because puu puu puu. I would edit the tributes entry but I don't really know, I'm not so good at wording stuff like that.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jul 16, 2014 11:47 am I'll edit the entry later today, or tomorrow.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jul 25, 2014 8:01 am I didn't like the pretending to be a hero part, did you really have to add that under the Orgun pic or are you just trying to be annoying. Also similar designs in other 80/early 90's, you could have sourced that reference. The guy who designed the Orgun mecha's also worked on many other shows before it, maybe it was one of those.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 25, 2014 10:19 am Hiroyuki Hataike was credited with one other mecha design role with the 90's OVA Devastator. The "similar designs" stuff is probably referring to a Bubblegum Crisis enemy suit that was sleek, purple and had a horn. But there's a lot of similar elements of Evangelions from other shows from the 80's and 90's. Anno said that the reveal of Eva-01 was taken right out of Combattler V. There's no need to source that statement, all designs borrow from previous designs, and they all eventually borrow from Go Nagai.

First take a look at these pics, notice similarities in their design. http://vanishingtrooper.files.wordpress.com/2012/07/orgun.
